title: Surveying and Mapping (except Geophysical) Services

narrative.executive_view: This is a real but narrow AI-value thesis: AI can remove a meaningful share of office processing while field observation, legal judgment, and responsible charge remain paid work. The stated EBITDA band is only 119 firms and is concentrated in 100-plus employee establishments, so it is not a broad small-firm roll-up universe. I therefore view it as a low-confidence, sub-scale platform thesis rather than a broad acquisition screen. [S1][S2][S4]
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: Technicians both collect measurements outdoors and enter, process, and map the data; BLS explicitly describes the mixed physical and digital workflow. AI and existing geospatial tools should help with data entry, image selection, drafting, map updates, research, and project administration, but they do not eliminate site visits, monument recovery, instrument operation, or accountable interpretation. The supplied OEWS mix is four-digit and engineering-heavy, so the role fractions are deliberately broad estimates rather than a reconstructed six-digit wage survey. [S1]
narrative.value_capture: Boundary work has a license-and-liability gate, but that gate requires responsible charge rather than passive stamping. Louisiana’s rule illustrates both sides: a licensed surveyor or person under responsible charge performs the boundary survey, and individual professional judgment remains required. That supports some retention of office savings on trusted repeat accounts, but bids for standardized work can pass savings to customers and a faster licensed reviewer can also put downward pressure on fees. [S2]
narrative.adoption_timing: There is evidence that drones and other advances improve efficiency while technicians remain needed to review and interpret output for accuracy and completeness. I use a long, uncertain 12-year remaining path to 50% effective AI-enabled workflow adoption from a low current base, not a claim that 50% of firms already use generative AI. This is a judgment about effective workflow penetration across field and office tasks, not a measured survey statistic. [S1]
narrative.consolidation_economics: The target count is constrained by the frozen size-band derivation. Bowman’s 2026 purchase of Smith & Associates is direct evidence of one active strategic acquirer of land-surveying capacity, and its 2021 filing records an earlier surveying-and-civil-engineering acquisition. Those facts establish buyer interest, not a broad competitive auction market or a transaction-multiple dataset; the selected 5.0x/7.0x planning multiples are conservative estimates with wide ranges. [S4][S5]
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid demand is DURABLE rather than immune. BLS expects technicians to keep gathering and preparing map information despite drones because accuracy and completeness still require review and interpretation. Boundary determination also remains subject to state licensure and responsible-charge rules. Unlicensed aerial mapping, repetitive deliverables, and office processing are materially more exposed, so durable demand may shrink toward field execution, accountable review, and client service rather than preserve today’s revenue per job. [S1][S2]
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: The largest uncertainties are the true six-digit role mix, adoption measurement, owner age, pricing mix, and transaction multiples. NSPS documents an aging profession and a shortage of younger licensed surveyors, but not an owner-age distribution; its evidence supports the succession-shortage flag but not a precise owner percentage. State rules vary, the supplied occupation cell is 5413 rather than 541370, and the market includes boundary, construction, and mapping work with very different licensing and pricing dynamics. [S3]

sources.title: BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook: Surveying and Mapping Technicians
sources.what_it_supports: Mixed field and office duties, use of drones/CAD/data processing, and BLS’s statement that technicians will still review and interpret technology output.

sources.title: Louisiana Administrative Code: standards for boundary surveys
sources.what_it_supports: Example state rule: boundary surveys require a licensed professional land surveyor or work under responsible charge and retain professional judgment.

sources.title: NSPS 2016 Hill Day Issues
sources.what_it_supports: NSPS states that the average professional surveyor age was 58 and retirements outpaced entrants; used only for documented workforce/succession shortage, not owner-age quantification.

sources.title: Bowman Acquires Nevada-based Smith & Associates Land Surveying
sources.what_it_supports: Direct evidence of a 2026 strategic acquisition of a land-surveying firm and reported $2.0m annualized net service billing.

sources.title: Bowman Consulting Group annual report acquisition note
sources.what_it_supports: 2021 acquisition note identifies 1519 Surveying, LLC as surveying/civil engineering and reports approximately $10.0m consideration; it does not report target EBITDA.

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: Field measurements and accountable boundary judgment create a floor, while office processing and generic coordination are exposed.
caveats: Supplied role mix is 4-digit 5413 and does not represent the core survey occupations cleanly.
Fractions are task judgments, not observed automation rates.

role_judgments.rationale: Four-digit civil-engineer contamination; documentation and analysis automate partially.
Four-digit architecture contamination; document work automates more than professional judgment.
Scheduling, reporting and coordination are partly automatable.
CAD and drawing production are office processing tasks.
Administration automates; client and operating accountability remain.
Engineering-cell proxy; analysis and documentation partly automate.
Management paperwork automates but responsible leadership remains.
Inspection-like field work retains physical and judgment requirements.
Engineering-cell proxy with partial analysis automation.
Software development can use AI coding assistance but retains integration work.
Unlisted tail is estimated to combine surveyors, crews, processing, and administration; physical and licensed work temper office automation.

pi_dist.rationale: License/accountability and repeat client relationships can retain part of savings, offset by bidding and standardized deliverables.
caveats: No US 541370 pricing-mix survey was located; this is not an observed billing mix.

pi_moat.rationale: Licensure/responsible charge and local client relationships offer a partial moat, while acquisition activity and local competition limit it.
caveats: Louisiana is an illustrative state rule, not a national market-share measure.

t50_years.rationale: BLS describes technology-assisted efficiency with continuing human review; 12 years is a conservative remaining-time judgment for 50% effective workflow adoption.
caveats: No defined US 541370 AI-adoption survey was located; this is not a measured diffusion curve.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Selected as a low estimate of material AI-enabled workflow penetration, separate from long-standing CAD, GNSS, and drone use.
caveats: BLS confirms technology use but does not measure generative-AI firm adoption.

historical_analogs.value: Existing drones/CAD/data-processing tools improved efficiency but BLS still expects human review and interpretation; no quantified 541370 diffusion duration was found.
historical_analogs.rationale: Qualitative analog only; it constrains the thesis against instant full displacement.
historical_analogs.plausible_range: Qualitative, not a numeric adoption-duration estimate
caveats: No dated, industry-specific adoption series was located.

selection.rationale: A 35% owner-over-60 planning estimate is below a majority and is informed only by NSPS’s aging-profession context.
caveats: NSPS reports professional-surveyor age, not firm-owner age or an age distribution.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: NSPS states retirements are outpacing new entrants.
caveats: Workforce shortage is a proxy for, not a count of, sale-ready successors.

active_consolidators.rationale: Count only Bowman, directly evidenced acquiring a land-surveying firm in 2026.
active_consolidators.plausible_range: At least 1; broader platform count not established here
caveats: This deliberately understates buyers not independently reopened.

buy_mult.rationale: Conservative planning multiple for a qualified smaller operator, below an assumed scaled-platform exit value.
caveats: Neither source provides target EBITDA; this is not a transaction-derived surveying multiple.
Owner compensation/SDE normalization is required in diligence.

exit_mult.rationale: Planning value assumes a modest premium for a scaled, integrated platform with recurring client relationships.
caveats: Not supported by a disclosed surveying transaction multiple; no public-comparable bridge was calculated.

pricing_structure.value: No quantitative US 541370 billing mix established; model as mixed fixed-fee, unit-price, and time-and-materials work with meaningful pass-through risk.
pricing_structure.rationale: Mixed field, office, and accountable professional work suggests varied contract structures, but no percentage split is claimed.
caveats: Do not infer a percentage pricing mix from this selection.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: A long remaining adoption period reflects slow effective deployment across field and accountable workflows; the V estimate describes eventual replaceable task share, not near-term realization.

terminal_value.justification: Boundary work is licensed/responsible-charge work in the illustrative state rule, and BLS expects continued human review and interpretation despite drones. Demand persists, while office and unlicensed mapping revenue face price pressure; no unsupported revenue split is asserted. [S1][S2]

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: No third-party market-size or engineering-broker multiple was substituted for 541370 evidence. The 4-digit OEWS occupation mix, state rule, workforce age, and corporate acquisitions are explicitly disclosed as scope-limited inputs.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: No US percentage billing mix was found. Fixed-fee or standardized outputs could pass office savings through, while physical field work and accountable review limit complete deflation; this remains a due-diligence question rather than a quantified claim.

dataset_mismatch.description: Role mix is 4-digit 5413, while this packet concerns 6-digit 541370; it overrepresents engineering/architecture relative to core survey roles.
sensitivity: AI-replaceable share may move materially if a 6-digit occupational wage mix is obtained.
The frozen n_band estimate selects only 100+ employee size classes, so it is not representative of the modal firm.

market_boundary.selection_basis: No reliable 541370 revenue split was located; terminal assessment is based on task and licensure distinctions, not a claimed dominant subsegment.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A ranges from 1.43 to 0.56; no industry-specific measured diffusion curve was located.
V is sensitive to the residual role composition obscured by the 4-digit OEWS cell.
M can disappear if scale and integration do not earn an exit premium.

reviewer_note: Text-first packet: conservative, scope-limited estimates; deterministic dataset and score arithmetic are intentionally left to the finalizer.
